name: Basic Red Chile Sauce
recipeInstructions: For a spicy sauce, use 1 New Mexico and 7 California chiles. Eliminate the New Mexico chile for a milder sauce. Wear gloves, remove the stems and seeds with a small knife, then wash the chiles well. Place in a saucepan with water and bring to a boil over high heat; boil for 5 minutes, then allow to sit for 10 to 15 minutes. Process the water and chiles in batches if necessary, using a food processor or blender, until finely chopped. Strain through a fine mesh sieve, pressing the pieces through with a wooden spoon. In a medium skillet, make a roux by combining flour and oil. Cook over low heat for 2 minutes, then add garlic, chile mixture, oregano, and vinegar. Stir well and simmer for 15 to 20 minutes. The sauce will keep in the refrigerator, tightly covered, for about 5 days or can be frozen for 2 to 3 months.
